channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Panama pledges to end key canal deal with China, work with US after Rubio visit
item.description: Panama's José Raúl Mulino announced that his country would not renew its agreement with China's Belt and Road Initiative following a visit from Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to speak with Trudeau, Mexico after imposing tariffs
item.description: President Trump said he would speak Monday morning with the leaders of Canada and Mexico after imposing a fresh round of tariffs on imports from those countries and made clear it would take significant action to lift the tariffs. “They have to balance out their trade, number one.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea slams Rubio for calling it 'rogue' state in 1st direct criticism of new Trump government
item.description: North Korea has lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned that such remarks will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trudeau, Sheinbaum vow to 'enhance the strong bilateral relations' amid US tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum pledged to “enhance the strong bilateral relations” between their two countries the same day President Trump enacted tariffs on them, according to a readout on Trudeau’s website.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: EU warns Trump it will 'respond firmly' to tariffs
item.description: The European Union (EU) said it will “respond firmly” if President Trump signs tariffs that could impact its member countries.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Canadian ambassador to U.S.: Canadians 'perplexed' and 'confused' by Trump's tariffs
item.description: Canada’s ambassador to the U.S., Kirsten Hillman, said on Sunday that Canadians are “perplexed” and “confused” by tariffs enacted by President Trump. “Canadians are perplexed, I think disappointed,” Hillman told ABC News’s George Stephanopoulos.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's trade war among allies triggers retaliation from Canada and Mexico
item.description: Canada and Mexico ordered retaliatory tariffs on American goods in response to sweeping tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies And Maria Verza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jailed ex-Pakistani PM Imran Khan draws parallels to President Trump in fight for justice
item.description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan looms large over the country's political scene, despite being jailed. Now questions arise as to whether President Trump could bring him some relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico, Canada announce retaliatory measures following Trump tariffs
item.description: Mexico and Canada are retaliating against President Trump's Saturday tariff announcements, enacting countermeasures of their own.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sick and wounded children begin crossing into Egypt as Rafah crossing opens after several months
item.description: RAFAH CROSSING, Egypt (AP) — A group of 50 sick and wounded Palestinian children began entering Egypt for treatment through Gaza’s Rafah crossing on Saturday, the first opening of the border since Israel captured it nearly nine months ago.
item.dc:creator: AHMED HATEM and AMR NABIL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Takeaways from the AP interview with Ukraine’s Zelenskyy
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Associated Press interviewed Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday at a crucial juncture of the nearly three-year war against Russia’s full-scale invasion. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, SAMYA KULLAB and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Italy takes back 43 migrants it planned to process in Albania in 3rd failed attempt to keep them out
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took migrants to Italy from asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome. It was the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the non-EU country.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he ordered airstrikes on Islamic State in Somalia
item.description: "These killers, who we found hiding in caves, threatened the United States and our Allies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Serbia's students lead Danube bridge blockades as protest draws tens of thousands calling for change
item.description: NOVI SAD, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's students led a mass protest and blocked bridges over the River Danube in the northern city of Novi Sad on Saturday, drawing tens of thousands into the streets to express their anger with the country's populist leadership and to call for change.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Venezuela agreed to accept migrants back, lauds return of hostages
item.description: President Trump said that Venezuela has agreed to accept the country’s migrants living in the U.S. back as he lauded the return of six American hostages who were held in the Latin American country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Sheinbaum offers condolences after 6 Mexican nationals killed in Philadelphia plane crash
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum issued a statement following Friday's fatal plane crash in Pennsylvania, which involved six Mexican nationals. "I regret the death of six Mexicans in the plane crash in Philadelphia, United States," she wrote on social platform X, according to translation.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: 18 Pakistani soldiers killed in fighting with separatist rebels in Balochistan
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani troops fought separatist insurgents who set up roadblocks in the restive northwestern region of Balochistan, leaving 18 paramilitary security forces and 23 rebels dead in some of the heaviest clashes in recent years, officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of Hamas’ youngest hostages is released
item.description: Yarden Bibas, father of Hamas' youngest hostage, has been released after more than 480 days in captivity, but his wife and two sons remain in Gaza. Their fates are still unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian drone and missile attacks kill 8 in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — At least eight people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities, local officials said Saturday. Meanwhile, Moscow’s troops continued their grinding advance through the country’s east.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbian anti-graft protesters march to a northern city and plan to block bridges
item.description: NOVI SAD, Serbia (AP) — Hundreds of students protesting graft they blame for 15 deaths in a building collapse marched Friday through Serbia to the northern city of Novi Sad where they plan to block three Danube River bridges this weekend.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels were quickly expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing Goma, the region's major city, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar’s military government extends its mandate to rule another 6 months
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar’s military government on Friday announced another six-month extension of its mandate to rule in preparation for elections it has said will be held this year, as the country enters its fifth year of crisis. However, it did not announce an exact date for the polls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Serbia rocked by anti-corruption protests after construction tragedy
item.description: Tens of thousands of people in Serbia have been marching in the streets to protest alleged Serbian government corruption after an accident that killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Taliban take over Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel, more than a decade after attacking it
item.description: The Taliban are taking over the operations of Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel in Kabul, more than a decade after they launched a deadly attack there that killed nine people. The Serena Hotel said Friday it was closing its operations in the Afghan capital on Feb.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's covert nuclear agency found operating out of top space program launch sites
item.description: Sources embedded in Iran uncover "large scale" personnel and operations affiliated with Iran's nuclear program are now operating out of two of Tehran's top space development and testing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Three Greek opposition parties vowed Friday to challenge the country’s center-right government with a censure motion over its handling of a deadly rail disaster nearly two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released Saturday, including the father of the youngest captives held in Gaza, brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu furious about chaotic handover of Israeli hostages from Hamas
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered a delay in the planned release of terrorists, seeking assurance regarding the release of hostages, but the prime minister's office later indicated that the demand had been satisfied.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
